stem: Let \( U \) be a universal set with \( |U| = [[U]] \), and let \( A, B \subseteq U \) with \( |A| = [[a]] \), \( |B| = [[b]] \), and \( |A \cap B| = [[ab]] \). By De Morgan's law, \( A^{c} \cap B^{c} = (A \cup B)^{c} \). Find \( |A^{c} \cap B^{c}| \).
solution: By De Morgan's law, \( A^{c}\cap B^{c} = (A\cup B)^{c} \), so \( |A^{c}\cap B^{c}| = |U| - |A\cup B| = [[U]] - ([[a]]+[[b]]-[[ab]]) = [[answer]] \).

This is \( |A \cup B| \) itself, forgetting to take the complement.
This complements only the intersection \( A\cap B \), not the union \( A \cup B \).
This complements only \( A \), ignoring \( B \) entirely.
This is off by one from the correct total.

stem: Sets \( A, B, C \) satisfy \( |A \cap B| = [[ab]] \), \( |A \cap C| = [[ac]] \), and \( |A \cap B \cap C| = [[abc]] \). Using the distributive law \( A \cap (B \cup C) = (A \cap B) \cup (A \cap C) \), find \( |A \cap (B \cup C)| \).
solution: \( (A\cap B) \) and \( (A\cap C) \) overlap in exactly \( A\cap B\cap C \), so by inclusion-exclusion on the union on the right-hand side: \( |A\cap(B\cup C)| = |A\cap B| + |A\cap C| - |A\cap B\cap C| = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to subtract the triple overlap \( |A\cap B\cap C| \), double-counting it.
This adds the triple overlap instead of subtracting it.
This multiplies the two intersection sizes instead of combining them by inclusion-exclusion.
This drops the \( |A\cap B| \) term entirely.

stem: Sets \( A, B, C \) satisfy \( |A| = [[a]] \), \( |A \cap B| = [[ab]] \), \( |A \cap C| = [[ac]] \), and \( |A \cap B \cap C| = [[abc]] \). Using the identity \( A \setminus (B \cup C) = (A \setminus B) \cap (A \setminus C) \), find \( |A \setminus (B \cup C)| \).
solution: \( A\setminus(B\cup C) = A \setminus (A\cap(B\cup C)) \), and \( |A\cap(B\cup C)| = |A\cap B|+|A\cap C|-|A\cap B\cap C| \), so \( |A\setminus(B\cup C)| = |A| - |A\cap B| - |A\cap C| + |A\cap B\cap C| = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to add back the triple overlap, subtracting it out too many times.
This subtracts the triple overlap again instead of adding it back.
This only removes \( A\cap B \), ignoring \( C \) entirely.

stem: Let \( U \) be a universal set with \( |U| = [[U]] \), and let \( A, B \subseteq U \) with \( |A \cap B| = [[ab]] \). By De Morgan's law, \( A^{c} \cup B^{c} = (A \cap B)^{c} \). Find \( |A^{c} \cup B^{c}| \).
solution: By De Morgan's law, \( A^{c}\cup B^{c} = (A\cap B)^{c} \), so \( |A^{c}\cup B^{c}| = |U| - |A\cap B| = [[U]] - [[ab]] = [[answer]] \).

solution: \( A \) and \( B\cap C \) overlap in exactly \( A\cap B\cap C \), so by inclusion-exclusion: \( |A\cup(B\cap C)| = |A| + |B\cap C| - |A\cap B\cap C| = [[answer]] \).

stem: Let \( U \) be a universal set with \( |U| = [[U]] \), and let \( A, B \subseteq U \) with \( |A| = [[a]] \) and \( |A \cap B| = [[ab]] \). Using the identity \( A^{c} \cup B = (A \setminus B)^{c} \), find \( |A^{c} \cup B| \).
solution: \( A\setminus B \) has \( |A|-|A\cap B| \) elements, and \( A^{c}\cup B = (A\setminus B)^{c} \), so \( |A^{c}\cup B| = |U| - (|A|-|A\cap B|) = [[U]] - [[a]] + [[ab]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to add back the overlap \( |A\cap B| \).
